We heard great reviews about Irvine Legal so we reached out to Joshua. He was very knowledgeable about the legal issues we were concerned with and we felt very confident in his ability to represent us. BEST decision we could have made.

He was very well versed in the law, tactful in his communication with the opposing party, but also stern/aggressive when it was needed. We quickly came to a resolution and didn’t end up needing to take this issue to court. -Though I’m confident we would have easily prevailed with him representing us

I HIGHLY RECOMMEND Joshua and his team at Irvine Legal to anyone needing legal assistance. My daughter was involved in a car wreck. After her accident, I felt completely lost and overwhelmed dealing with insurance companies and medical bills. With in 24 hours of the wreck, the insurance company offered an insulting low settlement number. I had no idea what to do it who to trust. I called several law firms, but I didn’t feel good about any of them. I saw the bill boards for Good Guys and decided to give them a call.  I’m so thrilled I did! From the moment I had my first interaction with them I felt relieved. They explained everything in a way I understood. I didn’t feel pressure to retain them, I felt confident!
From the moment I retained them, everything changed.
They took over all communication with the instance companies, they guided me through the medical staff, they offered several referrals to doctors, a but they were very willing to work with me on doctors I chose as well.
I love how one attorney and one paralegal saw my case through from the beginning to the end. There was no juggling me around to different staff members. I always knew who was working on my case, who to call when I had questions.
I had contact from them on a regular basis keeping me informed of the status. They were professional, compassionate, and committed to making sure my best interests came first!
I’m incredibly grateful for their help and I would highly recommend this firm to anyone’s who feels overwhelmed and unsure of where to turn after an accident!

They truly are the Good Guys!!!

I was convicted of a 3rd Degree Felony for Attempt to Distribute and Controlled Substance. I was living with a boyfriend. He was selling marijuana. Yes, unfortunately I knew and since it was just marijuana. I wasn't all too worried. I went with him since we were planning to go somewhere afterwards. He put the marijuana in my purse and made me count the money. Harmless right? Nope. I was pretty much an "accomplice" at this point. Booked a night in jail and thought Matthew Nebeker would help me. I was a full time student at SLCC and full time employee at AFCU. That changed after this situation. I was diagnosed with depression and anxiety. I am a good person. I run a fb group known as UTACURAZINE. We do donation drives to the humane society, road home and utah food bank. We also did car meets to donate blankets, sweaters etc during the winter for the homeless. This was on my own time and I did volunteer work at the humane society as well. That was not enough for Judge Dale to believe I was a good person. I have complied with every court date. I lost my job at AFCU and finding a different job with only experience with Customer service and having a felony. Was nearly impossible. I went from being a Teller to a Delivery Driver for an app. This was my first offense. I am only 22. I did my absolute best. All for just being at the scene and knowing what Jon was doing made me a drug dealer. I did not weigh the marijuana, bag it or anything. I did not deal with the undercover cop to set up times to meet up. I was not the one who was supposed to be in question. I was not the drug dealer. BUT during sentencing. Matt did not stand up for me. All he said was that I was a student and worked. THATS IT NONE OF MY VOLUNTEER WORK WAS NOTICED. I EVEN HAD THE PAPERWORK FROM ALL PLACES WE DONATED AND PHONE NUMBERS. Unfortunately, I was sentenced to 60 days home confinement. 2 years probation and 100 hours community service. WHICH WAS WORSE THAN WHAT JON WAS SENTENCED WITH. THE ONE IN QUESTION THE DRUG DEALER WHO EVEN ADMIT HE WAS THE ONLY ONE WHO WAS INVOLVED. Jon was only sentenced to 3 years probation and 60 days home confinement. With being on good behavior that probation time can be cut in half. So technically myself 1 year probation and Jon 1 year 6 months probation. How is that even possible? When it was my time to speak. I told the Judge everything about me. From being involved with the community. Even had a car meet ready to go clean up graffiti in the parks. I told him I was going to school and was almost finished. I am a good person to everyone around me and was a hard worker. I told him about where I've donated and even wanted to show him the papers for proof. Till he told me that he did not believe a word I said. He believed that I did have part in this. And according to all the paperwork he did not believe me. He said if I keep the mindset of not wanting to take blame. I would not be able to accomplish through probation. HIS LOSS THOUGH. My probation officer is amazing and even told me that I shouldn't of been sentenced so harshly. He was surprised they actually sentenced me at all. I am almost finished with probation and my po noticed my service. After we finished sentencing. I told Matthew that Jon did not get community service. Why did I? His answer, "Well, you do community service anyways right?" TERRIBLE I COULD NOT BELIEVE WHAT HE TOLD TO ME. Now he is suing me for the money that I owe him which is around 3k. Would you pay 3k to a lawyer who didn't even do his job? Nope and I won't. I don't care send it to collections. At least Jon's lawyer stood up for Jon and even argued his case. Matt was only paid to be there. If you have anything worse than a DUI I would suggest going somewhere else. I thought he can help me. I already have no money for the court fines, restitution, home confinement my cost of living etc. BUT HE HAS THE AUDACITY TO BELIEVE I HAVE MONEY FOR HIM? NOPE.

reviews.text:
I'm the legally appointed personal representative of my dear mother's estate. RIP Mom. I called this firm in 2019 for elder help. I suspected neglect and abuse of an elder, my dear mother, at Legacy House of Logan. My mother, sick with Alzheimer's, had been improperly taken there by her estranged offspring. No one in Utah believed me when I told them what had happened and that my mother was under the care of estranged-for-decades, disinherited-by-name offspring. I was still in communications with this firm when I was advised my mother died, in December 2019. This firm asked me if I was kidding when I told them she'd died. Rude. In 2020 I retained a local law firm and opened probate at First District Court. I subpoenaed records from the facility which reflect exactly my suspicions: isolation, abuse and neglect, in their records. I also have a Logan City police report reflecting the staff at the facility dissuaded LEO from making contact with my mother under my valid welfare check which contributed to her suffering. After her death, during probate, an already retired judge ignored rule of law and gave all my mother's estate funds to those who were disinherited-by-name. I was sole heir in my mother's legally prepared, uncontested will. I fought back under Reconsideration and took back what was improperly given away;  but my mother was victimized in Logan, and her legacy was dishonored and disrespected. My mother suffered and died in Utah, where she'd never lived. Then, I was subjected to an incompetent, conniving legal community that protects only their own ilk. Signed, Chrystina Bird, Loving Daughter & P.R., Estate of J.G. Pratt <3

Thanks for giving us the opportunity to solve your tax nightmare.

We appreciate client feedback, and always aim to provide the most effective service, and favorable outcomes, however some things are out of our control, and a one or two-star review is unwarranted, unjustified, and unkind. We took this clients case on in good faith, and we have been responsive at every step of the process.

Cases go through a cycle, and the cycle includes in-office review, client communication, client substantiation of a claim, and then submission of a request for relief.

I like to say we work fast, the IRS works slow. The variable here where we have little to no control is the timeframe of the IRS in responding to our requests for relief. The IRS is the most powerful collection agency on the planet, and they are also a bureaucratic organization, that works tediously slow. This then appears that we are working slow or are non-responsive, but the process in tax settlement negotiations is a type of first in first out process, and they are working cases on a severe backlog, with severe staffing limitations, and compound all of that with COVID shutdowns. All of these circumstances and considerations causes a process that typically takes 6-12 months to now take 12-22 months. Unfortunately, this is out of our control. All of this has been explained in multiple interactions.

The good news in all of this is that with the offer in compromise, an offer not responded to within 24 months is deemed accepted as submitted. We are as frustrated with the delay as our clients are, but we are responsive, and we are professional.

Our only concern, and our obsession is solving tax problems. While we feel it is unfortunate that Mr. Taylor feels the way he does, and that he felt inclined to leave a negative review, we still dedicate our practice to securing the most favorable outcomes for our clients. We look forward to a favorable resolution when the time comes and we are disappointed in the negative review because we feel it is unearned, inconsiderate of actual facts, and unfortunately shines a false light on a strong legal practice.

Of course we enjoy our "time to shine" in court, but why go through the time, expense, and all the bad feelings if you don't have to?  We always prefer to settle a case rather than litigate, even if it results in a mediocre review.  Resolving a case is cheaper, faster, and easier on both you and the kids.

Avoid Kelly White at All Costs!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I engaged Kelly White in June 2024 for representation in my divorce and custody case (Second District Court, Utah), with the expectation of robust advocacy to safeguard my daughters (ages 6 and 9) and my fundamental parental rights. Regrettably, my experience was characterized by egregious neglect, pervasive unprofessionalism, and an utter absence of case progression, leaving my family in a perilous and vulnerable state. The following substantiates why retaining Mr. White would be a grave miscalculation:

Complete Dereliction of Duty:

Notwithstanding urgent and substantiated concerns regarding my ex-wife’s overt custodial interference, calculated parental alienation, and her brother’s demonstrably dangerous conduct (e.g., exposing my minor children to a knife and a loaded firearm), Mr. White inexcusably failed to initiate requisite motions or petition the court for a safety evaluation. I meticulously provided compelling evidence, including a comprehensive report and an evidentiary recording, yet these critical submissions were ostensibly disregarded, thereby permitting my daughters to remain in an environment devoid of adequate safety measures.

Profound Communication Deficiencies:

Mr. White exhibited a consistent pattern of unresponsiveness and reactive engagement, rather than proactive counsel. My detailed correspondence dated June 17, 2025, requesting a clear case timeline and an actionable plan, elicited no reply whatsoever. In stark contrast to competent legal professionals who proactively guide their clientele, Mr. White relegated me to perpetually pursuing rudimentary case updates, fostering a profound sense of abandonment during a critically sensitive legal proceeding.

Unprofessional Threat of Withdrawal Amidst Culpable Delay:

When I legitimately articulated concerns pertaining to a protracted delay in the crucial custody evaluation—a delay demonstrably attributable to his profound lack of diligence in fulfilling procedural requirements—Mr. White, on June 19, 2025, unconscionably threatened to unilaterally withdraw his representation, stating, “I’ll file my withdrawal with the Court. Best of luck.” This declaration followed my advisement of a potential Bar complaint, unequivocally demonstrating a preference for disengagement over addressing his professional shortcomings. It is imperative to note that while Mr. White belatedly forwarded the requisite paperwork for a Custodial Evaluation on June 9th, 2025, my subsequent direct communication with the custodial evaluation team revealed a critical timeline discrepancy. The team had, in fact, transmitted the necessary documentation to Mr. White on May 17th, 2025, stipulating a mandatory submission deadline of June 2nd, 2025. Their experience mirrored my own: a complete nightmare and absence of professional engagement or response from Mr. White. In fact, they expressed relief upon learning of my formal complaint, confirming their own frustration with his unresponsiveness, as I had previously indicated to Mr. White I would pursue such action.

Exacerbated Detrimental Consequences:

Mr. White’s inaction has not only protracted the timeline of my case but has also actively jeopardized my daughters’ safety and compelled me to incur additional, unanticipated legal expenditures for competent new counsel, thereby amplifying my stress and financial burden. The unwarranted delay in the custody evaluation process may, regrettably, prejudice my case within the judicial framework.

I have previously encountered individuals who proffer specious assurances; however, Mr. White’s manifest neglect and dismissive professional demeanor are unparalleled. Preserve your financial resources and peace of mind—select an attorney who genuinely demonstrates commitment and delivers substantive results. I have initiated a formal complaint with the Utah Office of Professional Conduct and earnestly hope others can avert this distressing ordeal.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We appreciate the opportunity to respond, though we must do so carefully and ethically. As attorneys governed by the Utah Rules of Professional Conduct, we are strictly prohibited from disclosing confidential information—even when doing so would provide essential context and correct substantial factual misstatements in this review.
We understand the emotional intensity that accompanies family law matters, especially when custody issues are involved. These situations can understandably leave individuals feeling disappointed or angry when court outcomes do not match their personal expectations. However, our legal system is guided by evidence, procedure, and judicial discretion—not by allegations alone.
While we strongly disagree with the characterizations in this review, we are ethically constrained from sharing the underlying facts, communications, or court findings that would give a fuller picture. What we can say is that the court ultimately did not agree with many of the reviewer’s assertions, and we cannot ethically change, fabricate, or ignore facts or procedural realities to force a preferred outcome. We can only work within the framework of the law, facts presented, and the client’s own compliance with court requirements and deadlines.
We also want to emphasize that we do not believe in disparaging current or former clients, even in the face of public criticism. Our role is to advise, advocate within the law, and uphold professional standards—even when the process is difficult, and even when outcomes are not favorable.
We wish this former client the best in moving forward and hope that their next steps bring peace and resolution. Our firm remains committed to providing ethical, responsive, and competent legal representation to all clients, in accordance with both the law and the high standards of our profession.

Sincerely,

Sandberg, Stettler, & White (SSW Law)

Perhaps most importantly, Dustin consistently maintained a level, steady perspective. Dustin is one amazing attorney!! He helped me get through a contentious divorce and I ended up being vindicated on all levels. Dustin gave me hope that not all attorneys are out to get your money. We talked through our strategy together and I never once felt pressured to do anything. He was on target about everything that happened and I could not have chosen a better representative. Most importantly, Dustin has fantastic family values and really wants to do the right thing. If you’re wanting an attorney who can strategize for what is right…. Dustin is by far the right choice.

Dustin is definitely a fighter, but that doesn’t mean it’s in the best interest of someone’s kids. He definitely got a lot of money out of my ex, pushing him to fight every little thing. Which they lost in the long run. It drug out a situation for 3 years, putting my kids lives in limbo but also so much stress. He didn’t care what was best for his clients kids. An attorney should be a realist and know when to help you mediate a situation - not push you to fight, stonewall and cause heartache and wasted money. They should think of the children. I’m grateful to have attorneys who aren’t money hungry and know when to tell their clients let’s mediate this situation so it doesn’t drag on for your children or for your own sake. Attorneys should be thinking of all aspects if you truly want to help your client. That’s the healthy right thing.
